metadata.author: douglasswm
metadata.notes: Defective consumer goods within statutory warranty period. Tests Sale of Goods Act fitness-for-purpose + Lemon Law (CPFTA Part III).

raw_documents.content: I bought a Galaxy ZenPhone Pro from MegaMobile on 5 February 2026 for $1,299. Within 14 days the screen began flickering and the battery drained from 100% to 0% in under 3 hours despite manufacturer rating of 12 hours. I returned the phone on 22 February for repair. The store performed a software reset and returned it on 1 March, claiming the device was 'within spec'. The same defects recurred within 48 hours. I want a full refund or replacement under the lemon law.

MegaMobile's diagnostic report (ref: DG-2026-0412) shows no hardware fault. Battery drain is consistent with heavy app usage typical of new-device setup. The device passed all test scripts. We have offered a software recalibration under our 30-day satisfaction policy but the customer declined. We deny the device is defective.
Receipt #MM-2026-08712. Date: 5 Feb 2026. Item: Galaxy ZenPhone Pro 256GB. Price: $1,299. Manufacturer warranty: 12 months. Lemon law cover: 6 months from purchase under CPFTA Part III.

legal_rules: Consumer Protection (Fair Trading) Act 2003 Part III (Lemon Law)
Sale of Goods Act 1979 s.14 (satisfactory quality)
precedents: Speedo International v World of Sports
